name: Architext 1.3.0 Release Planning
summary: Release Planning lets users craft one specific next release from cherry-picked roadmap items and ad hoc manually entered items without creating a second planning model.

required.title: Release planning data contract
required.summary: Define how one target release plan maps selected roadmap items and manually entered ad hoc items into the existing Release Truth detail file, with optional source/date-added metadata but one shared release item shape.
required.rationale: Release Planning must help craft a specific next release while preserving Release Truth as the reviewed source of truth rather than creating a competing planning artifact.

required.title: Roadmap-to-release scope assembly
required.summary: Present roadmap items as a selectable list, let users add inline ad hoc items that become selected on submit, then create one next-release plan with a prefilled version and optional theme/title.
required.rationale: The next release starts by making the selected feature/fix list explicit and reviewable before implementation.

required.title: Approve release plan
required.summary: Let the maintainer approve a reviewed release plan so Architext writes the release JSON, regenerates release history, and updates the roadmap source for selected or newly added items.
required.rationale: User approval is the boundary between draft planning and accepted Release Truth state.

required.title: Release Truth Kanban view
required.summary: Add a Kanban projection grouped by planned, ready, in progress, blocked, deferred, and complete using the same Release Truth items.
required.rationale: Kanban is only an alternative projection of Release Truth items, not a second task tracker or planning model.

required.title: C4 container drilldown
required.summary: Let users click a C4 container and navigate to the component-level C4 diagram within that container when a matching view exists.
required.rationale: C4 views should support natural architecture exploration from context to container to component without requiring users to hunt through the view list.

required.title: Validate release plan data
required.summary: Validate planned release items, optional source/date-added metadata, dependencies, milestones, blockers, evidence needs, generated release history, and roadmap-source updates before the plan is accepted as Release Truth.
required.rationale: A bad release plan should fail loudly before it becomes accepted Release Truth or appears as healthy in the viewer.

required.title: Release planning LLM instructions
required.summary: Update managed LLM instructions so agents know how to help draft next-release plans, record roadmap/ad hoc item origins, surface prioritization and deferral decisions, and update Release Truth only after review.
required.rationale: LLM agents need explicit rules for drafting release plans so they do not silently choose scope, hide deferrals, or edit the wrong files.

required.title: Routing correctness pass
required.summary: Improve remaining dense-route failures, especially overlapping lines and readability issues found in complex Deployment and Data/Risks views.
required.rationale: Routing correctness is standing release scope until the remaining issues are solved or further gains are no longer worth the complexity.

required.title: Viewer refactor boundaries
required.summary: Extract Release Planning presentation UI from the main application file and keep Release Truth rendering policy in named presentation modules so future planning work no longer deepens the app shell.
required.rationale: Release Planning will add more viewer surface area; the viewer needs cleaner boundaries before that complexity lands.

required.title: CLI lifecycle refactor boundaries
required.summary: Move the CLI executable into a thin bootstrap and place lifecycle runtime behavior behind CLI adapter modules, including shared process, git, target-layout, and JSON filesystem boundaries.
required.rationale: Release Planning and data watching will touch lifecycle behavior, so lifecycle policy must stop accumulating in one executable file.

required.title: CI and release gate hardening
required.summary: Keep releasability verifiable through repository-owned fixtures, validation, build, benchmark, package dry-run, packed CLI smoke tests, and develop/main workflow checks.
required.rationale: The modified Gitflow process depends on CI proving develop aggregation and main release readiness.

planned.title: Data file watching and auto refresh
planned.summary: Have architext serve watch target data JSON files, wait for writes to settle, validate before refresh, and prompt before loading invalid updated JSON.
planned.rationale: Release Planning will create more frequent JSON edits, so local review should update without restarting the server.

planned.title: Release planning visual smoke coverage
planned.summary: Add browser smoke coverage for the full Release Planning interface: roadmap selection, inline ad hoc item creation, automatic selection, version prefill, optional title/theme, save/create, Kanban projection, details pane, and invalid-data prompts.
planned.rationale: Release Planning is interaction-heavy enough that unit tests alone will not protect the user workflow.

planned.title: Saved planning drafts
planned.summary: Persist release plans as draft Release Truth entries, keep them editable, show them in the release browser, and allow later approval without changing roadmap targets before approval.
planned.rationale: Draft persistence is now part of the reviewed planning workflow: it preserves work in progress without creating a second source of truth or making the draft current before approval.

stretch.title: PDF export
stretch.summary: Export the active Architext view as a portable PDF artifact for review, archival, or offline sharing.
stretch.rationale: PDF export is useful enough to keep visible for 1.3.0, but it should not displace Release Planning fundamentals.

stretch.title: Source extraction drafts
stretch.summary: Explore future source-file inspection that suggests architecture-data changes as review-only drafts; exact 1.3.0 target is TBD.
stretch.rationale: Source extraction means suggested architecture-data changes from source inspection. It remains TBD stretch scope because the first useful extraction target is not defined.

outOfScope.title: Hosted release planning service
outOfScope.summary: A hosted planning service is outside Architext's local-first, repository-owned model.
outOfScope.rationale: The release must keep planning local and data-owned.

workstreams.name: Planning contract
workstreams.summary: Keep Release Planning inside the Release Truth model and define proposal-to-release writes.

workstreams.name: Planning workflow
workstreams.summary: Assemble explicitly selected roadmap items and manually entered ad hoc work into reviewed scope for one target release.

workstreams.name: Viewer planning projections
workstreams.summary: Render planning state through Release Truth Kanban and focused details.

workstreams.name: Release plan validation
workstreams.summary: Keep accepted release plans deterministic, validated, and repairable through existing lifecycle commands.

workstreams.name: LLM release planning instructions
workstreams.summary: Teach LLM agents how to draft and maintain release plans without inventing side channels.

workstreams.name: Local iteration
workstreams.summary: Make local review respond to data changes quickly while preserving validation safety.

workstreams.name: Routing correctness
workstreams.summary: Carry routing readability forward as a recurring quality track for dense diagrams.

workstreams.name: Future export scope
workstreams.summary: Track stretch and future-facing export ideas so they remain visible without blocking the release.

workstreams.name: Viewer architecture
workstreams.summary: Release Planning and Release Truth presentation policy now live behind explicit viewer modules instead of expanding the app shell.

workstreams.name: CLI lifecycle architecture
workstreams.summary: The CLI executable is a bootstrap over adapter-owned lifecycle runtime and shared target-layout/process boundaries.

workstreams.name: CI and release gates
workstreams.summary: Keep develop aggregation and main release readiness covered by repeatable repository-owned checks.

workstreams.name: Source evidence drafts
workstreams.summary: Track source extraction as a stretch source of reviewable draft evidence.

dependencies.summary: Scope assembly must write into the approved planning contract.
Approving a release plan depends on having assembled candidate scope.
Kanban should render Release Truth items instead of defining its own model.
Validation should cover the writes performed when an approved release plan becomes Release Truth.
Auto refresh must keep invalid edits from replacing the last known good model.
Visual smoke coverage depends on the initial planning projection UI.
Kanban should build on the cleaned viewer component and presenter boundaries.
Data watching should attach to explicit serve lifecycle boundaries rather than deepen executable coupling.
Packed CLI smoke tests should verify the refactored lifecycle path.
